title: Performance Bonus
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Incentives & Bonuses › Performance Bonus
body: Performance Bonus LMCS distributes a bi-annual bonus based on daily reporting scores provided by Principals, combined with other school assessments. Bi-Annual Bonus Structure The overall bonus sum and category ratios are decided by the Governing Body. The overall sum is divided based on employee category: Teachers, Admin Staff, and Helping Staff . Payment Schedule Evaluated Months Bonus Payable With May, June, July, August, September Salaries of November, December, February & March (same year) October, November, December, February, March Salaries of May, June, July & September (next academic year) Bonus Allocation Split ½ of the overall bonus per category is a fixed bonus — equally divided amongst all employees in that category. The remaining ½ is performance-based , calculated on monthly performance scores. Performance Bonus Calculation Follow these steps to calculate the performance bonus for each employee: Sum all individual performance points across all employees in the category. Calculate the multiplier: Divide the total performance bonus pool by the sum of individual points, then round down to the nearest 10 . Multiply each employee's individual score by the multiplier to get their performance bonus amount. Worked Example Employee Performance Score Bonus (Multiplier = Rs. 30) Employee A 55 55 × 30 = Rs. 1,650/- Employee B 62 62 × 30 = Rs. 1,860/- Employee C 67 67 × 30 = Rs. 2,010/- Employee D 74 74 × 30 = Rs. 2,220/- Employee E 71 71 × 30 = Rs. 2,130/- Sum of Points 329 Overall Performance Bonus Pool = Rs. 10,000. Multiplier = 10,000 ÷ 329 = Rs. 30.39 → rounded to Rs. 30. Performance scores are assigned by Principals using the daily reporting format (MCR parameters). Consistent high scores across the evaluated period maximise the performance bonus component.

title: System of Increments
headings: Increment Due to Changes in Dearness Allowance (DA)
Changes in Increment Criteria w.e.f. 1st January 2020
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Incentives & Bonuses › System of Increments
body: System of Increments Fixed Annual Increment Salary increments are made with the salary of September every year, payable in October. Increments are made as per the rules and regulations of Government Institutions — typically 3% annual increment . Increment Due to Changes in Dearness Allowance (DA) A change in the percentage of Dearness Allowance may also affect the net pay. DA changes are decided by the School Management based on the financial performance and viability of the schools. DA changes are usually implemented with the first month's salary of the new academic year . Changes in Increment Criteria w.e.f. 1st January 2020 With effect from 1st January 2020 , at the time of joining, each new employee starts with 3 increments already applied to their base salary structure. New employees are subject to no further increments in the total Cost to Institution for the first 3 years from the date of joining. However, employees will experience an annual increase in Net Payable Salary during this period due to the diminishing percentages of the RRF (Refundable Reserve Fund) deduction each year. Increment Eligibility Rule Condition at time of September Increment Increment Applied (Salary Date − Date of Joining) > 36 months Increment + 1 (annual increment granted) (Salary Date − Date of Joining) ≤ 36 months Increment + 0 (no increment this year) The 3-year lock-in applies only to increases in the total Cost to Institution . The employee's take-home pay still increases each year as the RRF deduction percentage reduces (12% → 9% → 6% → 0% after 3 years).

title: Fee Clerk cum PRO – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Fee Clerk cum PRO
body: Fee Clerk cum PRO Usual working timings: 8:45 AM to 5:00 PM . Expected working days per year: 265 to 290 days . Fee Collection Duties Collect fees from all students between prescribed times as per school rules. Generate a report of fees collected on a particular day at the end of the day and present it to the financial head of the institution. Deposit fees collected on a particular day in the bank within 24 hours of collection. Obtain the financial head's signature in the ledger after showing proof of bank transaction via net banking. Check emails for administrative-related school works like NOC, last date for External Activities (EA), affiliation authority. Keep check on all day-to-day money matters: workshops, donations, fines, examination expenses, etc. Log all expenses into the Physical Day Book at the counter and on the Day Book Google Sheet every day. Collect an article from the Editorial Bench of the school after each event and submit it to the press reporter. Maintain all student records (student details, CCE mark updation, etc.) and report to the Principal and concerned authority. Administrative Duties (Working Days) Check emails for administrative-related school work: NOC, EA deadlines, affiliation, CCE mark updation. Maintain student details, parent details and contact information, marks, fees information, and certificates on the school software as well as in hard copy. Maintain teacher records on the software as well as in files. Issue various certificates: Transfer Certificate, Mark List, Migration, "To Whom It May Concern" letters, etc. Cross-check all related information including financial accounts before issuing. Working Days & Holidays One holiday per week — generally Sunday, but may be any day per school requirements. All gazetted holidays of Himachal Pradesh + 2 local holidays are given off. Compensatory leave may be claimed if called on a gazetted holiday. 1 Casual Leave per month (paid). In case of accumulation, leaves may be encashed at the rate of ½ day per accumulated leave. National holidays are expected to be working days. Expected to work for 265 to 290 days per year .

title: Games Teacher / PTI – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Games Teacher / PTI
body: Games Teacher / PTI The PTI must be present in the school campus at least 30 minutes before and after school . School timings: 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM . Stock Maintenance Maintain all sports-related stock and equipment. Keep an up-to-date inventory. Management of Helping Staff Ensure that helping staff perform their prescribed duties. Report irregularities to the Principal. Organise staff for preparation of major events (Investiture, Sports Day, House Competitions, Annual Function, etc.). Allot and administer helping staff before and after official school timing. Timetable Management Make timetable adjustments as per daily circumstances. Be aware of all adjustments of the day and act as a centre for information in this regard. Ensure teachers adhere to the timetable and discipline with the right spirit. During examinations, assign examination duties and ensure smooth conduction by arranging material in advance (sheets, maps, thread, etc.). Money Matters Deal with day-to-day money matters of the school and coordinate with office for Head Office communication. Discipline Act as a right hand to the Principal in managing and coordinating the school, staff, and students. Take disciplinary action against those who violate school norms. Conduct continuous rounds of the school to ensure discipline and cleanliness. Take care of general defaults — be strict on uniform, overall decorum, and English. Counsel students who are sent out by teachers. Physical Education At least 50% of allotted periods must be dedicated to theory for Classes XI and XII. Conduct fortnightly tests of theory taught. Set examination papers as per the school routine. Sports Competitions Liaise with sports authorities for coordination and sports functioning. Prepare sports teams for cluster games. May include sports practices on holidays and early mornings before school.

title: Helper – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Helper
body: Helper Reporting time: 7:45 AM Daily Duties Reporting time is 7:45 AM . Open the school premises and do dusting and sweeping/cleaning of the gate area, offices, and other specific areas instructed by the in-charge. After cleaning, be present around the gate area to receive students, listen to parents' issues pertaining to students, and inform the superior if needed. During morning assembly, the primary duty is to help the teachers. Perform prescribed duties in the Montessori Wing during school hours, along with washroom duty. Stamping notebooks during the day is also part of a helper's duty. Helpers are responsible for the safety, well-being, and discipline of students. Helpers must exercise responsibility especially during the dispersal time of the Montessori Wing and Primary Wing. Helpers assigned to school buses must perform bus duty as the conductor of the bus. Helpers must ensure they have lunch at different times so that the majority of helpers are available to teachers and students at all given times. Helpers must clean the Montessori classrooms between 1 PM and 3 PM. Facilitate the dispersal process of the senior students. From 3 PM onwards (after school), sweep and dust all classrooms. Ensure that the water supply is regular (switch on the motor at the right times). Responsible for watering plants regularly. Must adhere to tasks given by the PTI, Fee Clerk, Principal, and Teachers.

title: IT Teacher – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › IT Teacher
body: IT Teacher Marks Updation Check and compile CWA results on a fortnightly basis for parent-principal interaction. Compile FA & SA marks at the end of the respective time period. Assist teachers in the data entry process when help is required. Computer Laboratory & Hardware Maintenance of computer lab — hardware and software. Maintain and troubleshoot the EPABX system installed in the school campus. Look after and provide basic troubleshooting for CCTV cameras and all other hardware and software installed at the school. Disciplinary Duty Keep a check on the fine register and collection. Digital Classes Keep a check on all digital classes to ensure they are working properly. Update the digital class record regularly. Check the peripherals of digital classes and lab for proper working. Generate a Monthly Usage Report and present it at the Monthly Meet. Examination Rate the quality of papers submitted by teachers by email. Fix the formatting of papers before printing them. Help in the printing of examination papers. Social Media Responsibilities Facebook: Regular status updates and at least 10 photographs per day. Instagram: 1 photograph per day. Twitter: Status updates. YouTube: Regular videos of weekly events. Website: Choose the best 20 photographs every week and send for the website; provide regular data updates. Data Organisation Methodical daily storage of data (text, videos, photos, presentations). Compression of files and removal of slack files on a regular basis. General Teaching Responsibility The IT Teacher is responsible for the computer lab and all other IT-related jobs including digital classroom management, examination paper printing, and CBSE online updations. They must be ready to stay after school if required. External Assessment (DA) Set up the lab for DA and classes during morning assembly. Make DA schedule and circulate it in time to ensure smooth conduct. Undertake DA tests, giving a uniform time to all students. Computer Classes Take lab and activity classes after the end of each theoretical chapter. At the end of each semester, computers must be formatted and cleaned using Collin. Computers must be dusted monthly using a blower. At least 25% of periods should be taken as theory classes. Must stay updated with the latest developments in computers.

title: Peon cum Driver – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Peon cum Driver
body: Peon cum Driver Usual working timings: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM . Expected minimum working days per year: 220 days . Bus Duties (8–9 AM, 1–2 PM, 3–4 PM) Reach the bus starting point 5–10 minutes before departure for basic dusting and cleaning of the bus. Inspect the bus for signs of wear and tear. The driver is responsible for the assigned bus and must look after its service, documents, and maintenance. Drive at moderate speeds — no rash driving. Pick up students from allocated stops and patiently wait for students to board or alight. Use polite language with appropriate distance and body language — preferably English — with parents, students, and staff. Fill up the log book for the bus and attend to fuel requirements. Adhere to all norms of driving. In case of an accident, the driver must obtain compensation from the other party involved. Any shortfall is paid by the driver, as accidents are treated as negligence. Duties During School Hours Sit in the assigned corridor / place in the school, ensuring good discipline, and work as a helping hand to teachers and other support staff. Be instrumental in day-to-day jobs at the school: gardening, moving furniture, basic dusting, facilitating games, office running, and other school-related jobs. Must not refuse work assigned by the in-charge (PTI & Principal). For each refusal reported by the in-charge, a half-day's salary will be deducted. Lunch must be taken individually in the bus as per the schedule agreed upon by drivers. Must not use a mobile phone during school hours. Be ready to work extra on special occasions. Duties During Student Holidays Be aware of all documents for their respective vehicle and update them during holidays. Come to school before the beginning of session to help set up the school for re-opening. Must always be on call for school / school management requirements. In case of unavailability, a leave will be deducted. Outstation Duties Scenario Entitlement Outstation duty — no overnight stay Rs. 200/- for the day (all expenses) + bus fare reimbursement on submission of tickets Outstation duty — overnight stay Rs. 500/- per day (all personal expenses incl. food & stay) + bus fare reimbursement on submission of tickets School is closed on a day during the outstation tour 1 Compensatory Leave per off-day at school After completing any outstation tour 1 Compensatory Leave per completed tour Working Days & Holidays One holiday per week — generally Sunday, but may be any day per school requirements. All gazetted holidays of Himachal Pradesh + 2 local holidays are given off. The employee may still be called on a gazetted holiday; compensatory leave may be claimed for up to twice the working time, subject to approval by the head of the institution. Expected to be present for at least one-fourth of the duration of school vacations. The placement of working days within vacations is at the discretion of the head of the institution.

title: Principal – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Principal
body: Principal Core Responsibilities Supervise, guide, and control the work of teaching and non-teaching staff of the school. Carry out all administrative duties required of a head of office. Be responsible for the proper maintenance of school records, service records of teachers, and other registers. Oversee money-related handovers to the accounts in-charges. Handle official correspondence and furnish, within specified dates, returns and information required by the State Government / Board. Ensure that fines and misc. charges are realised and appropriately accounted for and duly appropriated for the purpose for which they were levied. Conduct physical verification of school property and stock at the beginning, end of the first semester, and at the end of the year — hence 3 times a year . Make satisfactory arrangements for good drinking water and other facilities for students. Give appropriate suggestions to the Coordinator to ensure that the school building, fixtures, furniture, office equipment, lavatories, playgrounds, and school garden are properly maintained. Be in-charge of admissions, preparation of school timetable, allocation of duties and teaching load to teachers, and provide necessary facilities to teachers in the discharge of their duties. Plan the year's academic work in advance in consultation with colleagues. Hold staff meetings at least once a month, review work done, and assess student progress. Help and guide teachers to promote their professional growth. Actively encourage participation in in-service education. Promote teachers' initiative for self-improvement and encourage educationally sound experiments. Supervise classroom teaching and secure co-operation among teachers of the same subject as well as inter-subject coordination. Arrange for special remedial teaching for children who need it. Plan and specify a regular timetable for scrutiny of pupils' written work and homework. Ensure timely and effective assessment and correction. Organise and coordinate various co-curricular activities through the house system. Send regularly the progress reports of students to their parents or guardians by means of Monthly Open Houses (MOH). Promote the physical well-being of pupils; ensure high standards of cleanliness and health habits. Devote at least 18 periods per week to teaching. title: Teacher – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Teacher
body: Teacher General Job Roles Act as a friend, guide, mentor, motivator, facilitator, preacher, and role model to students. Teach and facilitate learning among students. Be receptive to queries and clear them to the best of your abilities. Assess student progress through quizzes, tasks, projects, assessments, written and oral tests and assignments. Maintain a softcopy of marks on the School Software and a hardcopy record in the TPR. Dedicate a week or more for revision of the syllabus before each terminal exam. Communicate in the School only in English . Guide children on proper pronunciation and language skills. Maintain attendance for respective classes. Prepare Blueprint, Paper Setting, Marking Scheme and Solutions for examination papers. Undertake examination duty, followed by Checking, Super-Checking and marks updation on the School Management Software. Stay up to date on new trends and changes in the education sector through workshops, seminars, self-research, journals, and websites. Use audio-visual aids like Digital Classrooms to make learning a fun-filled experience. Ensure discipline within school premises. Prepare children to be future model citizens of the country. Educate students on etiquettes, health, and personal hygiene especially during lunch break. Impart student-centric education. Include students in remedial classes, especially under-achievers. Counsel students at various points of schooling. Organise outdoor activities like field trips, picnics, and games with an aim of providing holistic and practical education. Prepare students and fellow teachers for school events like Annual Functions, Investiture Ceremonies, Sports Days, and Morning Assemblies. Take classes (periods) as per the requirement of the school in the best interest of students. Hold various positions of responsibility like house moderator, activity in-charge, etc. Formulate a lesson plan and execute it as per CBSE curriculum while incorporating activities to challenge students' creativity. Maintain a TPR (Teacher's Personal Record) in detail. Update marks on the School Management Software along with a record in the TPR. Work as a good link between students, management, and parents. Maintain cordial relations with parents, fellow faculty, and management. Inform and guide parents about the progress of their children. Language Teachers Give special emphasis to the pronunciation of spoken language. Practise and evaluate Listening, Reading, Writing, and Speaking skills in the classroom. Work on students' writing skills; prepare appropriate content for the School Blog or magazine. (Sanskrit Teacher specific) Ensure correct Mantra Ucharna (chanting) pronunciation. Science Teacher Conduct teaching more by practicals than by textbooks; give hands-on experience alongside text-book teaching. It is recommended that Science is taught without the use of a physical textbook in class. Identify, help, and prepare students for various Science-based competitions and exhibitions. Main

title: Day Leaves
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Day Leaves
body: Day Leaves Day-based leave categories cover full-day absences. Each has different approval requirements and salary implications. Single Leave (Unpaid) A Single Leave is one unpaid working day off. It is granted when an employee has exhausted their Casual Leave balance for the year. Treated as an unpaid day — one day's salary is deducted. Requires prior written application and Principal's approval. If the leave is sandwiched around a public holiday (i.e., taken the day before or after a Sunday/gazetted holiday), the holiday is also counted as a Single Leave (sandwich rule). The sandwiched day becomes unpaid as well. Double Leave A Double Leave counts as two leave days for one calendar day's absence. It applies in specific circumstances: Maximum ⌊(total teachers at branch ÷ 10)⌋ Double Leaves may be active simultaneously at a single branch on any given day. If the limit is reached, additional applications will be deferred. Consecutive absences exceeding 6 working days trigger automatic classification as a Long Leave , which requires MD Operations approval and may affect service continuity. Absent An employee is marked "Absent" when they fail to report to duty without prior approval and without notifying the school. Rule Details Leave equivalent 1 Absent = 2 unpaid leave days deducted from salary Notification deadline Employee must notify the Principal by 6:00 PM the previous evening if they cannot attend the next day. Failure to notify = Absent (not Single Leave) Break in service 7 or more consecutive Absent days without communication = break in service . The employee may be treated as having abandoned their post, and disciplinary proceedings will be initiated Employees who are marked Absent repeatedly (more than 3 times in a term) will be counselled by the Principal. A fourth instance in the same term may result in a formal show-cause notice.

title: General Leave Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › General Leave Rules
body: General Leave Rules The following 11 rules apply to all employees across all LMCS branches, regardless of leave category. Leave is a privilege, not a right. All leave must be applied for and approved in advance. Unapproved absence is treated as "Absent" and carries a double-leave deduction. First-come, first-served. When two employees apply for the same date, the earlier application takes precedence. No two staff members in the same role at the same branch may be on leave simultaneously without prior approval from the MD Operations. One-day advance written notice required. Leave applications must be submitted to the Principal at least one working day before the intended leave date. Emergency leaves must be communicated by 6:00 PM the previous day (see Absent rules). Responsibility handover is mandatory. Before proceeding on leave, the employee must hand over all pending duties, registers, and responsibilities to a designated colleague. Failure to do so may result in leave being treated as unauthorised. External remuneration = marked on leave. Any employee who takes on paid work outside LMCS (private tuition, event hosting, freelance assignments, etc.) on a working day will be marked on leave for that day, regardless of physical presence at school. WOH 2-hour absence = half day. An employee absent for 2 or more hours during the Work-On-Hours (WOH) period — without prior approval — is marked as a half-day leave. Sanctioned leave does not guarantee pay. Whether leave is paid depends on the leave category (CL = paid; Unpaid Leave = unpaid). The employee must confirm the leave type with the Admin Manager before proceeding. Leave lapses on resignation. Any unused Casual Leave balance lapses on the date of resignation or termination. There is no encashment of CL at the time of leaving service. Leave during the first 3 months of service. Employees in their probationary period (first 3 months) are not entitled to Casual Leave. Any absence during this period is treated as Unpaid Leave. Sandwiched leaves. If a leave is taken on a day immediately before or after a holiday (Sunday, gazetted holiday, or school vacation), the intervening holiday is also counted as leave unless specifically exempted by the MD Operations. Leave records are maintained centrally. The Admin Manager maintains the master leave register. All branch Principals must report leaves by the end of each working day to ensure accurate records.

title: Leave & Vacation Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules
body: Leave & Vacation Rules LMCS has a structured leave system that balances employee welfare with operational continuity. Leave is a privilege, not a right, and is governed by the rules below. 📋 General Leave Rules 11 foundational rules that apply to all categories of leave 🎁 Leave Benefits Casual Leave and Compensatory Leave — eligibility, accumulation, encashment rules 📅 Day Leaves Single Leave, Double Leave, and Absent — rules and consequences ⏱️ Time-Based Leaves Late Leave, Short Leave, and Half Leave with time thresholds ⭐ Special & Work-Based Leave Special Day Leave, Special Day Absent, and Non-Compliance Leave 🏖️ Non-Teaching Staff Vacations OO/05/21 — vacation periods count as Casual Leave for admin/support staff

title: Leave Benefits
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Leave Benefits
body: Leave Benefits LMCS provides two categories of paid leave benefits: Casual Leave (CL) and Compensatory Leave. Both are governed by strict eligibility and usage rules. Casual Leave (CL) Rule Details Entitlement — Teaching Staff 1 Casual Leave per month (12 CL per academic year) Entitlement — Admin / Helping Staff ½ of every vacation day is counted as CL (see Non-Teaching Staff Vacations ) Nature Paid leave — salary is not deducted when CL is used Claim Deadline Must be claimed by 31 March of the academic year; unused CL lapses after this date Carry-Forward Not permitted. CL does not carry forward to the next academic year On Resignation / Termination Lapses immediately; no encashment of unused CL Half-Day CL A half-day leave taken against CL is counted as one full CL deducted from the balance Compensatory Leave Rule Details Eligibility Granted when an employee is required to work on a holiday or vacation day at the school's request Nature Paid leave — equivalent in value to CL Approval Chain Recommended by the Branch Principal → approved by MD Operations . Compensatory Leave is not valid without MD Operations approval. Encashment Not permitted. Compensatory Leave cannot be converted to cash Claim Deadline Must be claimed by 31 March of the same academic year; unused Compensatory Leave lapses after this date Employees are advised to track their CL balance monthly and plan leave accordingly. The Admin Manager publishes individual leave balance statements at the start of each term.

title: Non-Teaching Staff Vacations
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Non-Teaching Staff Vacations
body: Office Order Ref.: OO/05/21 Date: 4th February 2021 Subject: Vacation Period — Leave Treatment for Non-Teaching Staff Non-Teaching Staff Vacations Administrative staff, helping staff, fee clerks, and other non-teaching employees are expected to work during periods when teaching staff are on vacation. This office order defines how their Casual Leave accrues during such periods. The Rule For every 2 working days that a non-teaching employee works during a vacation period, they accrue 1 Casual Leave (CL) . In other words: ½ of the working days in the vacation period = CL granted . Worked Example Consider a vacation period of 26 calendar days : Item Count Total vacation calendar days 26 Less: Sundays and Saturdays (off days) −5 Less: Gazetted holidays within the period −2 Working days (non-teaching staff report) 19 CL accrued (½ of 19, rounded down) 9.5 CL Important Notes This accrual is in addition to the standard 1 CL/month entitlement for non-teaching staff. CL accrued under this order is subject to the same claim deadline (31 March) and no-carry-forward rules as regular CL. Non-teaching staff who are absent during a vacation working day will have that day deducted from their accrued CL for that period. The Admin Manager calculates and communicates the CL credit to each non-teaching employee at the end of each vacation period.

title: Special & Work-Based Leave
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Special & Work-Based Leave
body: Special & Work-Based Leave These leave categories are tied to school events, non-compliance, or special circumstances rather than a simple day-off request. Special Day Leave A Special Day Leave counts as 2 leave days and is applied when an employee is absent on any of the following high-priority school days: First or last day of an academic term Annual Function day Investiture Ceremony Sports Day External events where school representation is required Combined meetings called by the Principal or Head Office MD Operations meetings / all-staff briefings Absence on any of the above days without prior written approval from the MD Operations is automatically recorded as a Special Day Leave (2 leave days deducted). Special Day Absent If an employee is absent on a Special Day and fails to inform the school by the previous evening's deadline, the deduction escalates to 5 leave days . Scenario Deduction Absent on a Special Day (notified in advance) 2 leave days (Special Day Leave) Absent on a Special Day (no prior notification) 5 leave days (Special Day Absent) Non-Compliance Leave Non-Compliance Leave is deducted when an employee fails to complete an assigned responsibility, submit a required document, or follow a school process by its deadline — even if the employee was physically present at school. Rule Details Deduction per instance −0.1 leave per non-compliance instance Conversion 10 non-compliance instances = 1 full leave day deducted Frequency Calculated and deducted monthly from the employee's leave balance Examples of non-compliance Missing QP submission deadline, not updating CSM data on time, failing to sign registers, not handing over duties before leave Non-Compliance deductions are automatically flagged by the System Coordinator and forwarded to the Admin Manager. Employees receive a monthly summary of any deductions recorded against them.

title: Time-Based Leaves
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Time-Based Leaves
body: Time-Based Leaves Time-based leaves accumulate as fractional leave days based on the duration of partial absence. When sufficient instances accumulate, they convert into full leave deductions. Late Leave Rule Details Definition Arriving after the official reporting time Grace period Within 5 minutes of the official reporting time — not counted as Late Leave Conversion rate 4 Late Leaves = 1 full leave day deducted Recording Recorded in the branch register by the Principal and reported to the Admin Manager weekly Short Leave Rule Details Definition Leaving early or being absent for a part of the working day (less than a half day) Conversion rate 3 Short Leaves = 1 full leave day deducted Calculation Proportional to the work-day hours — each hour of absence within the workday is tracked. When 3 such instances are recorded, one full leave is deducted. Half Leave Rule Details Definition Absence covering approximately half the working day (typically the first half or second half of the school day) Conversion rate 2 Half Leaves = 1 full leave day deducted Minimum hours requirement The employee must have worked at least the minimum required hours on the day in question for it to qualify as a Half Leave rather than an Absent Summary Table Leave Type Instances to = 1 Full Leave Grace / Threshold Late Leave 4 Within 5 minutes = no Late Leave recorded Short Leave 3 Per-hour tracking; must be < half-day absence Half Leave 2 Must have worked minimum required hours

body: Office Order Ref.: HES/MD(Op.)/UK/OO/02/18 Date: 3rd October 2018 Subject: Outstation Driver Rules Outstation Driver Rules It has been decided by the Management that the following will be the terms and conditions for outstation drivers. Outstation Duty is one that does not include a visit to one of the Schools. Day Trip (No Overnight Stay) The driver will be entitled to Rs. 200/- for the day for all sorts of expenses. The driver will also be entitled to claim fare of bus travel, subject to the submission of Bus Tickets. Overnight Outstation Duty The driver is entitled to Rs. 500/- per day for all personal expenses including food etc. If the tour includes bus travel, the driver will be entitled to claim the fare, subject to submission of Bus Tickets. In a case where a driver is on outstation duty and the School of the driver is closed (Off-Day), the driver is entitled to 1 Compensatory Leave per Off-Day at School. The driver is entitled to 1 Compensatory Leave after each tour .

title: Frequently Asked Questions
headings: Can my child celebrate her/his birthday in School?
Is my child allowed to carry money to School?
What do I need to do to update any personal information?
How do I recover my child's lost belongings?
What if my child forgets to carry her/his lunch box?
What is the procedure if my child has to take leave of absence for a few days?
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › FAQ
body: Frequently Asked Questions Can my child celebrate her/his birthday in School? Yes, toffees / candies / chocolates costing less than or equal to Rs. 5 are allowed. It is further requested to NOT SEND 'Mithai' / presents, etc. for distribution to the Classmates and the Teachers. Is my child allowed to carry money to School? No. Children are not allowed to carry money to School, with the exception of when it is being asked by the Teacher. What do I need to do to update any personal information? Parents can either request for any type of change in information using the online portal at www.lms.org.in OR send an application to the respective Class Teacher for any kind of change in information viz. address, telephone / mobile numbers, email ID, Adhaar Card Information etc. How do I recover my child's lost belongings? 'Prevention is better than cure'. The name, class and section of the student should be clearly marked on all belongings of the students — e.g. blazers, sweaters, ties, belts, water bottles, tiffins etc. In case your ward has lost her/his belongings, please write to your child's class teacher and get the matter resolved. What if my child forgets to carry her/his lunch box? Don't worry. If informed in time, the Class Teacher ensures that your child will not go hungry . What is the procedure if my child has to take leave of absence for a few days? In case leave is required, the class teacher can be informed through a Leave Application which needs to be duly signed by the authorised parent / guardian. In case of an emergency, the Principal should be informed through phone / SMS / Whatsapp and the leave application must be submitted to the Class Teacher on joining.

title: Code of Conduct (Students)
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › Code of Conduct
body: Student Code of Conduct Young people who are given the privilege of joining the LMS fraternity should be aware that as members of this distinguished community, they carry the responsibility of upholding its ideals with dignity and pride. As the School's ambassadors, students are expected: To be kind, considerate and upright human beings, always ready to offer encouragement and lend a helping hand. To behave courteously to all, rich or poor, regardless of caste, creed, colour or religion, always respecting the liberty and rights of others. To look upon fellow humans from all parts of the world and all walks of life as brethren, avoiding rudeness and vulgarity, at all times. To make every effort to contribute their share in making the world a better place to live in, rather than idly gossiping, lamenting and grumbling over the evils they perceive. To possess the courage of their convictions, never hesitating to say a firm 'NO' when asked to do a thing, which they know to be wrong, however tempting it might seem. Never to be cruel, knowing that cruelty is the trait of bullies and low self esteem, while kindness is the mark of gentility and strength. To stand up for and personify the spirit of sportsmanship and fair play, never to lie, cheat or be deliberately unfair in order to win. Play the game with full zeal and accept defeat with good grace. To treat staff and visitors in a friendly and respectful manner, at School as well as at home, always rising to wish any teacher or guest politely and offering assistance voluntarily, without the need to be prompted. To accept assigned tasks and duties willingly and without argument, ensuring that these are carried out with the highest standards. To take pride in wearing their School uniform and strive to keep it neat and tidy at all times. To respect and treat public property with care; to look after the School premises with the pride of ownership; to prevent wilful damage whenever possible; and to promptly report any breakages observed. To take good care of themselves and adopt healthy habits, so as to remain mentally and physically active and strong enough to face the most difficult situations with courage and confidence.

title: Misconduct of Student
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › Misconduct of Student
body: Misconduct of Student The following escalation process applies for student misbehaviour and misconduct: On misbehaviour and misconduct in the Class the Teacher must give three warnings . If the teacher feels that she is unable to control the student, he/she should bring it to the Principal's notice by means of the TPR. After the third mention, the teacher can request the Principal to counsel the student. He/she must also inform the Parent only through a note in the Almanac about the Principal's Counselling. After the third counselling by the Principal, the teacher in consultation with the Principal can request for the Parent to be called for the MOH (Monthly Open House). If the student has not shown any improvement after the parents' third consecutive MOH, the Parent can be called to speak to the Principal. If the student continues to show signs of indiscipline and resistance to improve, the student can be called for a PTA meeting and addressed by the entire PTA Governing committee. In case the student still refuses to show any signs of improvement, the LMS Heads Forum can decide to suspend the student for a maximum of the rest of the Academic Term. In case the Student still refuses to change, the Heads Forum can decide to rusticate the student and the same shall be informed to the PTA Governing Body.

title: Rewards & Punishments
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › Rewards & Punishments
body: Rewards & Punishments 🔴 Red Star Careless and untidy work, indicating a general lack of effort in academics, may lead to the award of a Red Star by any teacher, which will be recorded on the Star Chart maintained in every classroom. 🔴 Red Card A student who gets 3 Red Stars , indicating a sustained lack of effort in academics, will be liable for the issue of a Red Card. The Head of the School, on the written recommendation of the Class Teacher(s), may issue a Red Card. This will be announced in Assembly and the name of the student will be posted on the School notice board. A student on a Red Card has to have the appropriate column in the card signed by the subject teacher concerned at the end of each period, on each day of the week. If progress during the week is satisfactory, the Head of School may discontinue the Red Card at the end of the week. 🟢 Green Star A Green Star, recorded on the Star Chart, will be awarded to a student for exemplary behaviour and performance within the classroom and the School campus. Any subject teacher can bestow a Green Star. Certificates will be awarded to students accumulating the maximum Green Stars in their respective class. 🟡 Yellow Star Yellow Stars may be given by Teachers for minor infringements of School rules and general misbehaviour. If a student gets 5 Yellow Stars she/he will be issued a Yellow Card. 🟡 Yellow Card Serious offences which adversely reflect a student's conduct — e.g., breaking norms, bullying, rudeness, insubordination, disobedience, cheating, telling lies — may lead to the issue of a Yellow Card. A Yellow Card is a serious matter. The offender is deprived of all privileges in the School for a period of one week. She/he is not allowed to attend any programme, picnic or outing while on a Yellow Card. A student on a Yellow Card must carry the card for the entire week's duration and is liable to have remarks entered on the card for any disciplinary infringement. At the end of the week, the student will meet the Head of the School. The receipt of two Yellow Cards within one academic year will lead to the expulsion of the offender from the School on disciplinary grounds.

title: School Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › School Rules
body: School Rules Every student must carry her/his Almanac to School every day and use it productively. Students who do not use school-arranged transport should ensure that they arrive at school at least 10 minutes before the first bell . The warning bell is rung 5 minutes before the start of the morning assembly. Students should move from one classroom to another between periods without wasting time, in an orderly and quiet manner. Students should be habitually clean and always neatly dressed. Non-Sikh boys should get their hair cut at regular intervals and ensure that it remains combed, short and tidy. No shouting or whistling is allowed in or around the School building. Running in corridors is strictly prohibited. It is strictly forbidden to throw any object at any one. Bullying and the use of foul language are punishable offences , whether in or out of School. Care must be taken of all School property and no student should scratch or spoil the desks or chairs, or damage any School furniture, or write or draw anything on the walls. Damage done, even by accident, should be reported at once to the School authorities. The cost of any wilful damage done will be made good by the concerned student, who will also be liable to pay a fine for any such offence. No irrelevant book (other than text books or library books), magazine or other publication may be brought to the School without the Class Teacher's explicit permission. Lending or borrowing of money or other articles is not permitted. Mobile phones, Audio and Video CDs, DVDs, Computer games are strictly banned in the School premises. Bringing these articles to School will lead to confiscation, never to be returned. Students who are escorted to and from School in the care of servants or domestic staff may not leave the School premises without their nominated escort. LMS students should always remember that the School is judged by their conduct outside the School premises , whether in or out of uniform. The School reserves the right to terminate the Schooling of students with unsatisfactory progress in studies or whose conduct is a bad precept for others. Exploding crackers or splashing colours during Diwali/Holi or any other occasion in the school premises or on School buses is strictly forbidden. Students must make every effort to be present for festivals and functions celebrated in the School which they are expected to attend. The School is committed to protect the environment and expects its students to be environment friendly in all their actions. Students should consciously avoid the use of polybags.

title: General Fees Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Fees & Administrative › General Fees Rules
body: General Fees Rules Fee Rules — La Montessori Chain of Schools 1.1 Payment Deadline & Late Fee School dues must be paid on or before the 10th of each month . Payment received after the 10th will attract a late fee as follows: Period Late Fee 11th – 15th of the month ₹100 16th – last day of the month ₹200 Every subsequent month of default ₹500 per month 1.2 Fee Counter Timings Day Timings School working days 9:00 am – 1:30 pm & 2:30 pm – 5:00 pm School holidays 10:00 am – 2:00 pm 1.3 Advance Payment & Annual Discount Fees may be deposited in advance for any number of months or for the entire academic year. A 5% discount on tuition fees is applicable to parents who pay the entire yearly tuition fee before 30th April . 1.4 Receipt Policy Important: Fees will be considered paid ONLY upon receipt of an official fee receipt (online or offline). NO RECEIPT = NO FEES PAID. Parents are requested to use the School's Online Fee Portal: https://onlinefee.lms.org.in/ Payment modes that do not generate an instant receipt — such as RTGS, NEFT, IMPS, UPI, cheque, GPay, Paytm, or PhonePe — are discouraged. If used, an immediate confirmation and fee receipt must be obtained from the Fee Office. 1.5 Authorised Payment Personnel Dues must be handed over only to the person at the fee counter . Payments made to any other individual will not be recognised. 1.6 Fee Heads & Transport Fee Tuition fees and conveyance fees are payable for 12 months of the academic year. Transport fee discounts based on days operated: Days Transport Operated Discount 0 – 5 days 50% discount 6 – 10 days 25% discount More than 10 days No discount 1.7 Inter-Branch Migration A migration fee is chargeable at the time of transfer. The fee structure of the destination branch will apply from the date of transfer. If the New Admission Fee paid at the original branch is lower than that of the destination branch, the parent is liable to pay the difference. No refund is applicable if the amount paid is higher. Fees already paid for the current academic year at the original branch will be credited towards the destination branch's fee structure. Any deficit is payable; any surplus is non-refundable. A transfer is considered incomplete until all fees as per the destination branch's fee structure have been cleared. 1.8 Default & Consequences Default Period Consequence More than 3 months Student will be debarred from appearing in examinations More than 6 months Student's name will be struck off from the school roll

title: New Admission Special Cases
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Fees & Administrative › New Admission Special Cases
body: New Admission Special Cases 1. Inter LMS Migration of Students A parent can transfer between the Branches of LMS by paying the difference in the New Admission Fee between the Original School Branch and the Destination School Branch. In case the New Admission Fees of the Original LMS Branch has increased, the latest New Admission fee will be considered to calculate the difference. 2. Returning Students In the case of a student returning back to LMS from another School, a 50% concession on New Admission Fees can be given. In the case of a student returning back to LMS from another School within 9 months of leaving the School, a 100% concession on New Admission Fees can be given. 3. Alumni Ward Acknowledging the significant bond between our institution and our esteemed alumni, we are pleased to extend a 50% discount on New Admission fees for wards of LMS alumni.

body: Parent Discounts & Incentives 2026–27 Ref.: HES/MD(Op.)/UK/OO/02/25 Date: 1st January 2026 OFFICE ORDER For the academic year 2026–27 the following provisions for Discounts and Incentives are approved and applicable. 1. New Parent Referral If "Parent A" refers "Parent B," both will receive a 20% discount on the new admission fees. If "Parent A" refers two or more new admissions, Parent A will get a 40% discount , and each referred parent will get a 20% discount. 2. Old Parent Referral Scheme An old parent is defined as a parent whose student is either active at the School or is an Alumni of the School. If an old parent refers a new admission, the referred new parent can get a 20% discount on the admission fees. The referring old parent will receive an incentive equal to 10% of the collected new admission fees as a discount on their tuition fees. If the referring old parent chooses not to offer the 20% discount to the referred new parent, they will receive 10% of the total admission fees paid by the referred new parent as a discount on their tuition fees. body: For Withdrawal & Transfer Certificate One month's advance written application or one month's dues in lieu thereof is required when the child is to be withdrawn. Fees is due so long as no written notice of withdrawal is submitted. Those who leave school in December must in all cases pay the fees for January and February of the coming year. title: Communication with School
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Starting at LMS › Communication with School
body: Communication with School Ways to Communicate There are several ways of communicating with the School: Primary Communication with the Teacher is through the Almanac . Parents can meet the Teacher between 8:45 am and 9:00 am on any School working day. Parents can visit School for Monthly Open House (MOH) as per dates given on a monthly basis by the Teachers. Parents can meet the Principal on Monday, Wednesday & Friday between 10:00 am and 11:00 am on School Working Days. Parents can call the Principal directly on his/her personal mobile number between 6:00 pm and 8:00 pm on all School Working Days. Parents can WhatsApp, SMS or email the Principal at any time and can expect a response within 24 hours . School Open House will be held for Half Yearly and Final Exams. Parents and Students' interest is paramount; in case of any emergency the above norms can be waived off as per the Principal's discretion. School Email Communications The School sends frequent emails to students on their official email ID. The following can be expected through Email: Majority of the information and notifications are sent through email. Daily Home Work is sent through email. Question Papers for CWA, OTT, SA (Examinations). Report Card. Outsourced vendor assessments.

title: Use of an Almanac
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Starting at LMS › Use of an Almanac
body: Use of an Almanac An Almanac aims at establishing an invaluable link between Teachers, Students and Parents. It is intended to serve multiple purposes: As an all-in-one ready reckoner and planner for students. As a means of communication between Parents and the School. To keep Parents informed about their child's progress at School. To keep Parents informed with respect to assignments for home. To serve as a handy reference with regard to the student's schedule. To be used for convenient, day-to-day correspondence (related to Academics and Conduct) between Parents and the School. Student's Role Almanac must be brought to School daily. Must read all information in the Almanac and abide by it. Must update the Home Assignment daily. Performance Report should be updated simultaneously. Students of Class VI & above must give timely feedback on their teachers. Parent's Role Review the current page daily and must countersign. Check & sign Communication Record on page 4 daily. Check & sign Performance Reports at the end of the Almanac regularly. Use Almanac as the primary source of Communication with the Teacher.

title: Examination & CWA Rules
headings: Rules of Examination & CWA (Class Work Assessment)
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Examination & CWA Rules
body: Rules of Examination & CWA (Class Work Assessment) Academic Year: The academic year will be divided into two semesters from March to February . Class Work Assessment will be conducted regularly. Report cards will be given to the parents at the end of the session. Rules of Examination & CWA Attendance is compulsory for all tests and examinations held during the year, failing which the student will be given a zero . No consideration will be given to absentees, except on medical grounds, at the sole discretion of the Head of the School. It is the responsibility of both students and their parents to ensure that they do not miss any Written Tests or Examinations. A minimum attendance record of 90% of the total number of working days is compulsory. Even on medical grounds, a child remaining absent for more than 20% of the total number of working days in each term, may not be allowed to give examination. There is no provision for re-testing students, who remain absent on the day of the examination, or those who have failed in one or more subjects. Students reporting late for examinations will not be given any extra time for answering the question papers. Students found using any unfair means during any of the examinations, will be given zero in that subject. A repeat of such cheating in the future will result in the prompt issue of a Transfer Certificate, without notice. The answer scripts of the examinations will be distributed to students after evaluation. Errors in evaluation — such as mistakes in totalling, unmarked answers etc. — must be brought to the notice of the concerned Teachers instantly . Delaying this, may result in ignoring the same while preparing the final result. Requests for the issue of Progress Cards before the declaration of results will not be entertained.

title: Extra Curricular Activities
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Extra Curricular Activities
body: Role of Extra Curricular Activities Extra Curricular Activities and sports at LMS are as important as the Education itself . Extra Curricular Activities, Sports & Competitions ensure a holistically developed child. The Activities, Sports & Competitions are essential because they help students attain essential 21st Century Skills : Critical Thinking & Problem Solving Creativity & Innovation Collaboration Communication, Information & Media Literacy Cross-Cultural Understanding Computing & ICT Literacy They make the students confident and self-reliant. A lot of the activities are designed to help students get acquainted with possible skills, hobbies and interests. A number of competitions at School are derived from interview and evaluation centres, laying the foundation for competitive exams like UPSC, SSB, IBPS, CAT, SSC, etc. These also help students explore non-academic career options. How Competitions are Organised School Teachers mentor students about various types of extra curricular activities. A competition is usually held on a Tuesday at the Class level , where teachers give feedback and select the top 3–5 students for the School Level Competition. Students have a day to improve on the feedback. A School Level Competition is then held on a Thursday .

title: Negative Impact of Private Tuition
headings: Negative Impact of Private Tuition Classes
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Private Tuition
body: Negative Impact of Private Tuition Classes Tuition Classes in most cases are like slow poison for the School Students. The tuition classes can destroy a student's Critical Thinking & Problem Solving Powers . The Students also become dependent on other people for their own work. Tuition Classes either replace the Self-Study time or the Play time . Reduction in either of these have been known to have a negative impact on the development of the student. The Tuition Classes also take a toll on a student's family time . They have also been known to reduce the creativity of a student. In the senior classes, most students are known to acquire all sorts of bad & negative habits during the travel to the tuition classes.

title: Reading Habits
headings: Reading – The Most Precious Gift for Your Child
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Reading Habits
body: Reading Habits & Its Nuances Reading – The Most Precious Gift for Your Child Reading is the most important skill children ever learn in School and one best strengthened by the untiring efforts of enthusiastic parents. Learning to read takes time and effort. Reading is not something children can just pick up — children need encouragement to work at it and to keep trying if they get frustrated. Reading is the doorway to a lifetime of learning. Children learn the nuts and bolts at School, but they still need the special one-to-one attention only parents can provide. Reading together must be a top priority in your daily agenda. Reading Tips Start today. The earlier, the better. Use the VAKT method : Visual, Audio, Kinaesthetic and Tactile — multi-sensory teaching techniques using all five sensory organs. Run your finger below the words as you read aloud. Show how you begin reading from the top and work down. Point out repetitive words. Ask children to spot these words. Talk about pictures. Unfamiliar words can be figured out by looking at pictures. Alternate story-reading sessions with days when your child narrates the story back to you. Beyond Books Children should have opportunities to go places and do different things. The more they experience, the easier it is to read because of new ideas and vocabulary they are exposed to. Get children involved in everyday reading — directions, grocery lists, recipes, labels, instructions, road signs. Subscribe to children's newspapers. The ability to listen and not merely to hear is a prerequisite to developing holistically. Limit gadgets during the initial 3 planes of Education. Encourage children to pen down their thoughts. Keep pencil crayons and paper handy. Approach to Reading A balanced reading programme should employ phonics — teaching children how to sound out words. The four key language skills to develop: Listening Skill Speaking Skill Reading Skill Writing Skill

title: Role of Homework
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Role of Homework
body: Role of Homework Homework is a constructive tool in the teaching/learning process, geared to the needs and abilities of students, for the reinforcement of matter learnt in School. It is also intended to help students develop independent study habits . Student Responsibilities Students should try and do their homework entirely on their own, without the help of their parents. They should develop a regular homework routine, keep their parents informed with respect to homework assignments and show them the finished work. Students should: Ensure that they are prepared with whatever stationery is needed for homework tasks. Give their parents sufficient notice, if any extra materials are needed for projects and other such home assignments, so that these can be acquired without inconvenience. Create, with the help of their parents, an appropriate environment at home for quiet study without disruption. Earmark a regular block of time for homework. If no homework is assigned they should maintain the routine, by reviewing class work or reading a book during the time assigned for homework. Ensure that their homework assignments are completed in time and that all supporting books and materials are carried back to the School. Seek assistance from their parents only when specifically required, as per instructions from their teacher. Ask their parents, on a daily basis, to review their homework tasks, show them the completed work and ensure that they sign the Almanac. Seek their parents' help to list the things they did not understand, so that they can share these with their teacher at School, the next day.

title: Absence from School
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Transport, Attendance & Timing › Absence from School
body: Absence from School A student, returning to School after suffering an infectious or contagious disease, should produce a doctor's fitness certificate permitting her/him to attend School. Parents will be promptly informed in the event of serious illness or emergencies and may be requested to come to School to take their ward home if the case is serious enough to merit their attention. Repeated absence without leave or unexplained absence for more than six consecutive days renders the student liable to have her/his name struck off the rolls. Re-admission may only be granted after payment of a re-admission fee. All students are expected to attend School on the opening day immediately following the vacations. Absence for more than three days after the vacations end, will attract strict disciplinary action . It is compulsory for students to complete 90% attendance (80% in case of medical reasons) in each term in order to be eligible to take the end-of-the-term examination. This stipulation can only be relaxed on genuine medical grounds, at the sole discretion of the Head of the School.

title: Transport Facility
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Transport, Attendance & Timing › Transport Facility
body: Regarding Transport Facility (Conveyance) Students are expected to follow the given norms: Conveyance once availed cannot be terminated for the current year. Buses will not wait for late comers. No student should travel standing on the footboard. Students must not move around in the bus when it is in motion. No student should come near the entry door of the bus until it comes to complete halt. The front door of the bus is the only authorised entrance and exit. Boarding and alighting from buses should be done in silence and in an orderly manner. Students must make sure that the aisle of the bus is clear, and that school bags and other belongings are placed properly. Students must keep all parts of their body inside the bus at all times. They should not put their hands out even for waving. No object should be thrown inside or outside the bus. The consumption of eatables and drinks is not permitted in the bus. Unruly behaviour like shrieking and shouting is strictly prohibited; courteous behaviour is expected at all times. The driver's attention must not be distracted for any reason. The drivers are authorised to stop buses at designated stops only , unless otherwise directed by the bus teacher. The bus teacher and the monitor on duty are responsible for maintaining discipline in the bus. Any serious offence will be reported to the Head of School immediately. Parents/guardians of students who do not avail the School's transport should present themselves at the School gate 10 minutes before the start/dispersal of the School to drop/collect their wards.
